text: Guadalupe Cattle Company Facility • New Braunfels, Texas
1:00 PM to 6:00 PM Viewing of Sale Cattle
On all females purchased in this sale R2 Ranch will give 2 units of semen from any
Red Brangus Reference Sire they own listed with an asterisk on Reference Sire Page.
April 26, 2003 • R2 Ranch headquarters near Staples, Texas
Every heifer purchased by a junior member from the R2 Ranch program is eligible to compete
at this exclusive show. The R2 Ranch Junior Jackpot is an annual event designed to
benefit junior members of the Red Brangus and Red Angus Associations.
A total of $18,000 will be awarded to junior members at this event
SEE DIRECTIONS TO SALE AT END OF TERMS & CONDITIONS
Gulf Coast Cattleman E. C. Larkin Bill Lefty
Weekly Livestock Reporter Ralph Means Roseville, California
for further information contact: Sale Day Phones
800 R2 Ranch Road • Kingsbury, Texas 78638 210/389-3008 (210/389-3008, 1-866-Redcows • email: cdmarburger@hotmail.com)
1107 Cottondale Road • Wharton, TX 77488 Holiday Inn • 830/625-8017
I would like to welcome anyone interested in Angus Plus, Red
Brangus or Red Angus to our Second Annual “Source for Success”
Sale. I am happy to say that this sale is heading in the direction, I
planned a production sale. Most of the cattle being sold, were born
and raised at R2 Ranch. Next year, I hope to say that almost all were
bred here as well. I am very excited about the future of red cattle,
particularly those with some ear. I want to raise cattle that will be
capable of doing more with less. I feel we are starting to make
progress in this direction. It will take us years to get where we are
going. The M&M cattle we purchased from the Morrows are capable
of doing as much if not more than any other red cattle I have seen.
But some of them need more input than I am going to give them. The
Red Angus cattle that I purchased from Roy Beeby are low mainte-
nance, but some of them do not produce enough and some cannot
tolerate the heat. As we continue to cross and combine these lines
and others from Glacier Red Angus, Section M Ranch, Dogwood
Creek, McClanahan Ranch, Buffalo Creek, Beckton, Leachman,
Shamrock Nook, Swaner Brahmans, and many others, we plan to
develop the fertile, heat-tolerant, easy-keeping, high-grading red
We have had many changes in the last year. I would like to welcome
our Cattle Operations Manager, Doug Marburger and his wife Gay. I
know I speak for all of us at the ranch when I say that we are very
happy and very lucky to have Doug come join us. We have a new
sales manager this year John Joyce of “J” Custom Fitters and John
has been a great help in pulling this all together. As always all of us
are available to assist you in any way we can.
Choice of Two Red Brangus Cows
BW: 80 • Ratio: 105 • Adj WW: 744 • Ratio 119 • Adj YW: 1144 • Ratio 109
EPD's BW 4.54 • WW 30.4 • YW 57.1 • Milk 3.6 • Total Maternal 19.1 • BW Maternal -0.25
EPD's BW -0.82• WW 24.2 • YW 32.3 • Milk 9.5 • Total Maternal 21.3 • BW Maternal 1.39
LOT 1 is your choice of two great Red Brangus cows M&M Lisa 139/9 or M&M Ms. 201/41.
Lisa 139/9 was the Grand Champion female at Houston in 2000. She is a Golden Boy daughter with
style and capacity to spare. Lisa's dam was Miss WF 113/9. She was another one of the most produc-
tive cows in the Morrow's herd. Unfortunately she died calving in the fall of 2001. We have two sisters
of Lisa's retained in the herd. Lisa is one of the great 1/4 blood donor prospects.
201/41 has a phenomenal production record, all of her calves that were in large contemporary groups
have ratioed over 110% for weaning weight. Her first calf was a daughter, 201/6, who we still have in the
herd. That plus having her twin sister are the only reasons we would consider letting you take home
201/41. Low birth weight with milk and growth 201/41 is what we are all looking for. 201/4 is a 33/64
Brahman eight year old donor cow.
M&M Golden 3/0 is a premier 1/4 blood herd sire. He has sold over 1000 straws of semen in South America. He is a thick,
meaty bull, who will put muscle and performance in your calves. We are selling full possession and 99% semen interest.
An outstanding John Wayne daughter that was Champion at the 2002 San Antonio Junor Show AHE Catati Lady 745
and Reserve Champion at Houston in 2002. An example of the tremendous job this bull does.
EPDs: BW 2.6 • WW 28 • YW 45 • Milk 7 • Total Maternal 21
John Wayne is an eight year old 100% Red Angus herd sire. John Wayne's Red Brangus progeny have excelled in the show
ring and the pasture. He had the Grand Champion Red Brangus get of sire in Houston in 2001. His son M&M Little John
30/8 was National Champion bull in 2000. His daughter 574/0 was Junior Grand Champion heifer in San Antonio in 2002
for Charlotte Atkinson. His progeny work in the show ring and inthe pasture. We intend to continue to use him heavily. We
own 2/3 interest and full possession and are offering to you today 1/2 interest and full possession.

Spring 2002 Red Brangus Show Heifer Prospects
Lots 17, 18, 19, 20 & 21are all calves from RA Brown None Better 413. This was Bruce Monroe's senior Red Angus herd sire at
Section M Ranch. This bull worked. We could have put any of his daughters in the sale. Unfortunately the bull was injured and is
no longer alive. These five and the bred heifers from him we are selling later will probably be your only chance to get these genetics
that work. Section M cattle are fertile, easy keeping mothers. The kind we all need. And these 5 heifers are showy to boot.

Except for those stated in the terms and conditions below, there are no warranties, either expressed or implied, as to the merchantabil-
ity or fitness for a particular purpose with respect to the cattle being sold at this auction. The warranties and guarantees set forth in
these Terms and Conditions are in lieu of all other warranties, either expressed or implied, and the remedies provided herein shall be
the sole and exclusive remedy for the Buyer, or any party claiming through the Buyer, for any breach of warranty or guarantee herein
provided, and all other obligations or liabilities.
a) The following terms and conditions of sale are adapted from those recommended for use by all members of the Red Angus
b) Copies of those terms and conditions of sale are available on request from the Red Angus Association of America.
All cattle in this sale are offered according to the laws of the State of Texas. The right to bid, as provided under law, is reserved
for all sellers unless otherwise announced.
1. BIDDING: The auctioneer in charge will settle any disputes as to bids, and his decision on such matters shall be final.
2. TERMS: Terms of sale are cash unless satisfactory credit arrangements have been made with Sale Management prior to sale.
3. PURCHASER’S RISK: Each animal becomes the risk of the Buyer as soon as sold, but it shall be the obligation of the Seller to
see that animals are fed and cared for free of charge to Buyer, until loaded for shipment or until the expiration of 48 hours after
4. CERTIFICATE OF REGISTRY: A transferred certificate of registry will be furnished to the Buyer for each animal.
5. HEALTH: All animals are eligible for interstate shipment, except as otherwise announced. Interstate health papers will be
furnished for each animal requiring tuberculosis and brucellosis tests in accordance with Federal Regulations.
6. A.I. / NON-OWNER BULL PERMITS: A.I./Non-Owner Bull Permits shall be furnished by the Seller, at no cost to the Buyer,
for all females bred artificially or naturally to bulls not owned by the Seller.
7. ANNOUNCEMENTS: Any changes from information of any kind in this catalog will be announced from the auction box and
a) All animals are guaranteed by the Seller to be breeders, with the exception of:
b) Calves under 12 months of age.
ii) Animals shown by the purchaser after the sale. The breeding guarantees, if any, shall be as agreed upon between Seller
iii) Gross negligence or will misconduct on the part of Buyer.
b) Bulls are guaranteed breeders if not allowed to run within herd until at least 14 months of age. Any bull that either, (1)
settles one-third or more of the healthy cows he serves, (2) is the sire of 20 or more animals, or (3) settles cows by natural
service and passes a fertility test made by a competent veterinarian during any six-month period of trial (provided for in
paragraph 10b of Options and Privileges of Return or Adjustment, in this section) shall be considered a breeder. Any
guarantees with respect to the ability to freeze semen shall be by separate agreement between Buyer and Seller.
c) Safe in Calf Females have been examined by a competent veterinarian and are so guaranteed.
d) Served females are believed to be in calf but are not so guaranteed.
e) Pasture-bred Females have been exposed but are not guaranteed to be safe in calf.
f) Open females have not been served and are so guaranteed.
9. IDENTIFICATION: Seller offers each animal with readable tatoos or brands corresponding to its registration.
10. OPTIONS AND PRIVILEGES OF RETURN OR ADJUSTMENT :
a) All claims for adjustment or refund must be made in writing within six months of the sale date or not later than six months
after the animal reaches 18 months of age, with the exception of claims involving defects of parentage.
b) In the event an animal is claimed to be a non-breeder, the animal may be returned to the farm/ ranch of the Seller if in good
condition and complying with the health requirements of the Sellerís state. The Seller shall be entitled to six months trial
following the return of the animal in which to prove the animal is a breeder. If at the end of six months the Seller is unable to
prove the animal is a breeder, the Seller, at the option of the Buyer, shall replace the animal with another of equal value or
refund the purchase price. The return of the full purchase price shall in any case be deemed full satisfaction and settlement.
Any expenses incurred for transporting an animal claimed to be a non-breeder shall be the responsibility of the Buyer; except
that the Seller shall be responsible for transportation cost in excess of the distance between the Buyerís farm/ranch and the
location where the sale took place. If the Seller proves the animal to be a breeder, it shall be the obligation of the Buyer to
take delivery of the animal and pay all expenses incurred for transportation.
c) If a female sold as safe in calf proves not to be in calf, the Buyer may receive service from the bull previously used. If the
bull is not available, the Seller shall be obligated to make a satisfactory adjustment to the purchase price upon return of the
animal to the farm/ranch of the Seller. Any adjustment provided for herein shall absolve the Seller from further liability,
except in the case such female proves to be a non-breeder. If a female represented as ìsafe in calfî at time of sale proves to
have been bred to a different bull, the Seller shall be obligated to make a satisfactory adjustment on the purchase price upon
return of the animal to the farm of the Seller (adjustments shall not be considered to be a breeding fee or an allocation of the
purchase price to any unborn calf). Any expense incurred for transportation shall be the responsibility of the Buyer, except
that the Seller shall be responsible for transportation cost in excess of the distance between the Buyerís farm/ranch and the
location where the sale took place.
11. DEFAULT: In the event the Buyer defaults on payment, the Buyer shall be responsible for all costs, including attorney's costs,
incurred by Sale Management or Seller in connection with collecting, or attempting to collect, payment due.
a) All animals are guaranteed by the Seller not to produce calves with the following defects: DWARFISM, DOUBLE MUS
CLING, PARROT MOUTH, OSTEOPETROSIS (marble bone disease) and SYNDACTYLISM (mule foot). The parentage
of all animals bred by the Seller is guaranteed to the extent that the animal is by the sire and out of the dam that is indicated
on its registration paper. These guarantees are effective for a period of two years from date of sale only under these
i) The Buyer shall notify the Seller in writing immediately upon birth of calf suspected of being defective or immediately
after determination is made that such calf is defective, or if parentage is suspect or found to be in error.
ii) The Seller, at his expense shall be permitted, and provided with reasonable opportunity, to obtain blood samples of
alleged defective calf and its sire and dam for the purpose of DNA or blood type analysis to establish parentage. In the
case of suspect parentage, the Buyer, at his expense, shall be permitted, and provided with reasonable opportunity, to
obtain samples of the animal in question and its sire and dam for the purpose of DNA or blood type analysis to establish
parentage. If sire and dam, as indicated on the animalís Registration Certificate, are proven to be in error by such
analysis, Seller shall reimburse Buyer for cost of such analysis.
iv) The animals will be returned by the Buyer to the Seller at the Buyerís expense, provided the animals meet the health
v) The replacement of an animal with another of equal value or the refund of the purchase price in connection with a
defect producing animal or an animal whose parentage is in error shall be deemed full satisfaction and settlement.
vi) In the event there is a dispute as to whether or not a calf is defective, or parentage is in error, determination referred to
in above, shall be made by a person or persons acceptable to both the Buyer and Seller, and in the event the Buyer and
Seller cannot agree on the selection of such a person or persons then each of them shall appoint a representative to
make the determination. In the event that such representatives fail to agree, a third person selected by the represen-
tatives of the Buyer and Seller shall make the determination.
13. SELLER'S SEMEN RIGHTS: Any semen rights retained by the Seller must be announced from the auction box at the time of
the sale, if not already printed in the sale catalog.
a) The above terms and conditions of sale shall constitute a contract between the Buyer and Seller of each animal and shall be
equallybinding upon both. Each sale or resale of an animal constitutes a separate transaction.
b) Unless specifically stated herein, neither the Sale Management nor the Red Angus Association of America shall have any
liability for any event or occurrence related in any manner to this sale. All persons who attend the sale do so at their own
risk. The Seller, the owner of the sale facility, and sales manager assume no liability for accidents that may occur.

If you are flying to the sale you are 20 minutes from the San Antonio Airport and 45
minutes from the Austin Airport. Call us if you need transportation from either airport.
From San Antonio Airport take IH 35 North to New Braunfels take Hwy 46 (towards
Seguin) - Sale Facility 2 1/2 miles on right and from Austin take IH 35 South to New
Braunfels take Hwy 46 (toward Seguin) - Sale Facility 2 1/2 miles on right.
